title: Fixed-Length, Close-Delimited, Absent, and Incomplete Content · Execution Trace: Premature Eof Incomplete
summary: A body reader allocates from attacker Content-Length, treats premature EOF as success, consumes the next pipelined message, and reuses a connection after incomplete content. Planned artifact: Add and verify the premature eof incomplete increment in bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify EOF before the required octet count as incomplete framing and prevent dispatch or connection reuse.
Stream exactly the validated content length through independent policy limits without allocating the declared size.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Premature Eof Incomplete · Theory Premature Eof Incomplete
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 5916 in a archival client parsing incomplete close-delimited responses. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate exact-length, incomplete, and response-only close-delimited content; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then change certificate identity while ALPN stays valid and reject the connection. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Premature Eof Incomplete contract for the supplied data.
